title: erigeron ramulosus
intro: Erigeron ramulosus, also known as branched fleabane or branching fleabane, is a perennial herb native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation and promoting healing, as well as its ability to act as an antispasmodic to alleviate cramps and spasms. Erigeron ramulosus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and it is often used in herbal remedies to soothe and calm the skin and muscles.
